title: Struthiopteris exaltata
taxonomy: Struthiopteris exaltata is classified as a species of fern. It belongs to the genus Struthiopteris, which is part of the family Blechnaceae. The family Blechnaceae is a group of ferns commonly known as the chain ferns. The order Blechnales is also associated with this family. Struthiopteris exaltata is a part of the class Polypodiopsida, a division of vascular plants.

intro: Struthiopteris exaltata, also known as the struthio fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Blechnaceae, characterized by its unique, tall-stemmed ferns with divided leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Struthiopteris exaltata include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties.
